steps.title: Find the contrast signal
steps.content: The passage says Primary took a different approach, which signals a contrast with the earlier idea that some bands pick unique names to stand out.

steps.title: Apply the contrast transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship is contrast: most bands choose unique names to stand out, but Primary did the opposite — they named themselves after an existing band's song. The blank needs a transition signaling opposition.
  • Choice A — therefore,: signals cause-and-effect, so fails — Primary's choice is not a result of what other bands do. ✗
  • Choice B — by contrast,: signals opposition, so fits — Primary's approach directly opposes the strategy just described. ✓
  • Choice C — for example,: signals a specific instance of the general claim, so fails — Primary contradicts the general claim rather than illustrating it. ✗
  • Choice D — secondly,: signals a second item in a list, so fails — no first item has been enumerated. ✗
B is correct.
